summary: Dual-use Chromium credential extraction tool with obfuscated license module
details: Package advertises itself as a Chromium credential extraction utility: a native addon (addon.cpp) attaches Windows debugger APIs to running Chrome/Edge/Brave processes and uses DPAPI/CryptUnprotectData against the browser's Local State to extract AES keys and decrypt cookies and saved passwords. All operations run locally against the operator's own machine; no network exfiltration is present in the shipped code paths. The package also ships a bytenode-compiled, js-confuser-style obfuscated module (src/license.jsc) and the native addon exposes a setLicenseHash export, indicating an out-of-band license-gate. The main entry index.js requires './index.jsc', which is absent from the published tarball, so require('@ibrahim1337/baksen') currently fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND and no obfuscated code executes via the documented load path. Routing to human review because this is offensive-security/dual-use tooling whose intended user is also the operator (consent assumed when run against one's own browser), combined with shipped obfuscation that cannot be inspected, but no installer-side harm flow (no remote exfil, no install-time RCE, no credential redistribution) is observable in this version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
